title: Cole Tomas Allen, Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Suspect, Was Propelled by Outrage, Authorities Say
summary: A man who has worked as a tutor and graduated from the California Institute of Technology is being held by authorities in connection with the armed attack at the White House correspondents’ dinner.

title: How The Times Covers Attackers, Suspects and Victims of Violence
summary: Reporting on the people who upend life and those whose lives are upended can bring surprising and uncomfortable details to light.

title: Supreme Court Wrangles With Police Use of Cell Location Data to Find Suspects
summary: Geofence searches allow law enforcement to find suspects and witnesses by sweeping up location data from cellphone users near crime scenes.

title: Supreme Court to Hear Arguments in Landmark Roundup Weedkiller Case
summary: A victory for the manufacturer, Bayer, could end thousands of lawsuits against the company claiming that the herbicide causes cancer.

title: Iran War Shakes Global Economy, but the U.S. Has Mostly Been Spared
summary: In just eight weeks, much of the global economy has been knocked sideways. America has mostly been spared from the tumult.

title: China’s Economy Starts to Show Cracks From Iran War
summary: China’s strategic reserves of oil and natural gas have insulated it somewhat, but its manufacturing-based economy is beginning to falter.

title: King Charles Is Coming to the U.S. at a Very Delicate Time
summary: Not since his mother, Queen Elizabeth II, traveled to Washington after the Suez Crisis has a visit by the British monarch come at such a fraught point in Anglo-American relations.

title: 8 Memorable Moments From Past British Royal Visits to the U.S.
summary: With King Charles III and Queen Camilla set to arrive for a state visit with President Trump, we look back at some major moments in previous royal trips to the United States.

title: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
summary: A new therapy has the potential to cure hundreds of diseases — and even reverse aging.

title: Sergey Brin Moves to the Right, With a ‘MAGA Girlfriend’ by His Side
summary: After once backing liberal causes, the Google co-founder has praised President Trump, donated to Republicans and spent $57 million to try to block a California billionaire tax.

title: Ron DeSantis Aims to Add Four Republican House Seats in Florida Redistricting Push
summary: The Republican-controlled Legislature is meeting in Tallahassee this week to vote on the map, which would apply for the 2026 midterms if passed.

title: Virginia Court Weighs Legality of New Redistricting Map Approved Last Week
summary: Oral arguments on Monday morning lasted about an hour. It was not clear how justices would rule.

title: How the Trump Administration Ended Independent Science at E.P.A.
summary: The agency’s prestigious research office spent decades doing scientific work insulated from political pressure. Now it’s being dismantled.

title: EEOC Is Prioritizing Job Discrimination Cases That Match Trump’s Agenda
summary: Field staff at the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission say they are being pressured to bring politically charged cases, even with little evidence.

title: Judge Delays Order to Force Penn to Turn Over List of Jews to Trump Administration
summary: The Trump administration had said it needed the information for an antisemitism investigation.

title: Best New Orleans Restaurants
summary: There’s plenty of great Creole and Cajun food, of course, but also outstanding Vietnamese, Indian and Trinbagonian cuisine.

title: The Best Los Angeles Restaurants
summary: Our current favorites in one of the greatest food cities in the world.

title: Best Miami Restaurants
summary: The culinary capital of South Florida has outstanding Cuban and Caribbean cuisine — of course — but also world-class Japanese, Italian, Ethiopian and more.

title: Canada Setting Up Sovereign Wealth Fund
summary: Canada’s sovereign wealth fund will be far smaller than ones in other oil-producing nations like Norway and the Middle East.

title: Canada’s New Route to Citizenship Has Thousands of Americans Lining Up to Apply
summary: Canada has opened a route to citizenship for people who can prove they have a Canada-born ancestor. Millions could qualify, and Americans are already lining up to apply.

title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman Bring OpenAI Trial Spectacle to Oakland
summary: Oakland, Calif., where Elon Musk’s trial against OpenAI and its chief executive, Sam Altman, starts on Monday, is not exactly known as a hub of the tech industry.

title: From Indiana to Idaho, a Backlash Against A.I. Gathers Momentum
summary: The widening movement is pulling in people from all walks of life, united by a worry that Big Tech will cash in while average Americans bear the costs.

title: Canada Says Its Gold Is Traceable and Clean. So We Traced It.
summary: We ended up in a Colombian mine controlled by a drug cartel.

title: U.S. Mint Buys Drug Cartel Gold and Sells It as ‘American’
summary: As prices for the precious metal soar, the industry’s guardrails have broken down.

title: How We Traced U.S. Government Gold to a Drug Cartel
summary: Three reporters followed supply chains to reveal that the U.S. Mint buys gold that comes from foreign pawn shops and drug dealers, then claims it is from the United States.

title: Eric Swalwell, Tony Gonzales and Accountability in the Post-Post-#MeToo Era
summary: What constitutes unacceptable behavior? And how many accusers does it take for an allegation to be believed? Societal standards remain nebulous.

title: Are Movies Really Getting Darker? Let’s Shine Some Light on the Issue.
summary: Movie watchers are often left wondering why today’s films look murkier than films of years past. Here’s how some experts diagnose the issue.

title: Thanks to GLP-1s, Obesity Experts Are Trying to Understand ‘Food Noise’
summary: Before the rise of GLP-1s, obesity experts didn’t study the internal buzz that compels people to eat. Now that food noise is being switched off, they want to understand it.

title: Trump’s Shockingly Unqualified U.S. Attorney Picks
summary: Revenge-based criminal cases against the president’s enemies have not come to fruition. That’s where the U.S. attorneys come in.

title: Gold Standards
summary: We explore a Times investigation into the gold market.

title: Inside a Once-Grand Building That Housed the Vulnerable for a Century
summary: A shelter for men near Bellevue Hospital is closing. It is a symbol of an approach to homelessness that the Mamdani administration hopes to leave behind.

title: Human Remains Found in Tampa Bay in Search for Missing USF Student
summary: The remains have not yet been identified, but a former University of South Florida student has been charged in connection with the murders of Nahida Bristy and Zamil Limon.

title: The Rise of the High-Range, Less Expensive E.V.
summary: Even as the electric vehicle market has slumped, there are more long-range E.V.s under $40,000 than ever before.

title: Missouri and Illinois Face Severe Storms and Tornado Threat
summary: Areas around St. Louis could face tornadoes, forecasters said. Dangerous weather has unfolded across the Midwest in recent days.
